summary: Decompiled most of src/melee/it/items/itheart.c, reaching a reported 97.89% file match for the Heart Container item. The PR added the Heart Container state table and behavior routines, introduced HeartContainerVars fields needed to access heal amount, flags, and rotation/fall attributes, corrected related item prototypes and a BobOmbRain field type, added HSD_JObjAddRotationY, and marked many now-static itheart-local functions and float constants as local in symbols.txt. One function, it_80283C48, remained low-scoring and used awkward bitfield/byte access, with review discussion explicitly noting bitfields as the blocker.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; added 200 lines implementing Heart Container spawn/setup, state callbacks, animation transitions, rotation updates, fall physics, and reference handling.
Expanded HeartContainerVars with xDD8 flags/bitfield union, xDDC, xDE0 filler, xDE8_float, and xDEC.
Exposed global Heart Container declarations including it_80283AE4 and it_80283BD4 and included it/types.h for Item_GObj.
Corrected several related prototypes, including it_80283AE4 from HSD_GObj*/s32 to Item_GObj*/Item_GObj* and other argument type fixes.
Changed struct BobOmbRain.x0 from enum_t to HSD_GObj*, apparently to align with related item function prototypes/types.
Added static inline HSD_JObjAddRotationY with assert, rotate.y increment, and matrix dirty update respecting JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT.
Changed itheart helper functions and two sdata2 float constants from global to local scope.

what_changed: Replaced the essentially empty itheart.c with implementations for Heart Container routines at it_80283AE4 through it_80284194.

Added ItemStateTable it_803F56C8 with five state entries using static callbacks such as it_80283CD4, it_80283DA4, it_80283DA8, it_80283DFC, it_80283ECC, it_80283EFC, it_80284020, it_80284028, it_80284154, it_8028415C, and it_80284160.

Implemented it_80283AE4 as a Heart item spawn helper using SpawnItem, It_Kind_Heart, copied/zeroed position and velocity fields, Item_80268B18, GET_ITEM, HeartContainer.xDD8.bits.b0, HeartContainer.xDDC, and Item_80267454.

Implemented Heart Container initialization routines that copy special attributes into item variables, set vertical velocity from xDE8_float, set xDD4_heal, clear/set xDD8 bit b0, and transition to item animation state 1.
Implemented state callbacks that use HSD_JObjGetChild(GET_JOBJ(gobj)), HSD_JObjAddRotationY(child, attrs->xDEC), Item_80268E5C, it_8026B390, it_80272860, it_8026D62C, it_8026E15C, and it_8026E8C4.
Implemented reveal/reset animations that clear JOBJ_HIDDEN, set rotation Y to 0.0F, and switch animation states 2, 3, or 4.
Added HeartContainerVars layout beyond xDD4_heal, including a bitfield/flags union at xDD8, xDDC, xDE0_filler[8], xDE8_float, and xDEC.
Added HSD_JObjAddRotationY inline to baselib jobj.h, mirroring existing JObj translation-style helpers but for rotate.y and dirtying the matrix unless JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT is set.
Corrected item header prototypes to use Item_GObj* or HSD_GObj* where the previous declarations used s32 or less-specific types.
Updated symbols.txt so many internal itheart callbacks and it_804DC8A8/it_804DC8AC constants are local rather than global.
smart_moves: Kept internal state callbacks static in C and synchronized their symbol scope to local, reducing exported symbol noise and matching their use solely through the local state table.
Modeled HeartContainerVars.xDD8 as a union of named bitfields and a raw u32 flags member, allowing both bit-level access and full-word copying from special attributes.
Factored repeated child-JObj rotation into a reusable HSD_JObjAddRotationY inline instead of open-coding rotate.y updates in item code.
Used existing item framework helpers such as Item_80268B18, Item_80268E5C, it_8026D62C, it_8026E15C, and it_8026E8C4 rather than inventing new behavior paths.
Improved adjacent header types while matching itheart, especially changing prototype arguments from s32 to GObj pointer types where the implementation evidence required it.
decomp_lessons: When matching item files, build the ItemStateTable early; it exposes which callbacks are local/static and often drives symbol-scope cleanup.
Special-attribute structs should be expanded only as far as the matched code proves offsets and usage; here xDD4, xDD8, xDDC, xDE8, and xDEC were directly exercised by itheart.

Bitfields can be useful for readable flag access, but they may generate difficult codegen; the remaining low-score it_80283C48 and review comment show that raw byte/flag access may be necessary for exact matching.
Header prototype correctness matters for matching: replacing placeholder scalar parameters with Item_GObj*/HSD_GObj* can remove casts and align call signatures across item files.
Inline baselib helpers should preserve engine invariants, including HSD_ASSERT and matrix-dirty behavior, rather than just mutating the struct field.
Use local symbol scope for static callbacks and local constants once a C file owns them; this keeps the symbol map consistent with the decompilation.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-based names such as it_80283AE4, it_80283CD4, and it_80284160.
Item state table kept address-based object name it_803F56C8.
Struct fields retained offset-style names such as xDD4_heal, xDD8, xDDC, xDE0_filler, xDE8_float, and xDEC where semantic names were not fully known.
Bitfield flags used generic b0 through b7 naming under an xDD8.bits member, with xDD8.flags for raw access.
SpawnItem and Item fields followed existing project names such as kind, prev_pos, facing_dir, x3C_damage, x0_parent_gobj, x4_parent_gobj2, x44_flag.bits.b0, and x40.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used static forward declarations for local callbacks before the ItemStateTable so table initialization could point at functions while keeping C symbol scope local.
Matched zeroing of vectors with explicit assignments such as spawn.vel.x = spawn.vel.y = spawn.vel.z = 0.0F and setting prev_pos.z to 0.0F.
Used HSD_JObjGetChild(GET_JOBJ(gobj)) before rotation/hide operations, matching the item model child manipulation pattern.
Used callback arguments to transition helpers, e.g. it_8026D62C(gobj, it_80283DD4), it_8026E15C(gobj, it_80283C7C), and it_8026E8C4(gobj, it_80283C7C, it_80283DD4).
For the difficult it_80283C48, used explicit casts through s8* and raw flag masking/shifting to test xDD8 flags and index into gm_80473A18, indicating a low-level workaround around bitfield codegen.
Marked functions at 80283C7C through 80283EFC, 80284020/80284028, and 80284154/8028415C/80284160 local in symbols.txt after implementing them as static.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.interpretation: Reviewer/maintainer considered helping with the remaining mismatch, likely the low-scoring it_80283C48 shown in the report.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.interpretation: The remaining matching issue was tied to bitfield code generation; evidence supports caution when using C bitfields for exact matching.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully matched it_80283C48 or changed HeartContainerVars.xDD8 bitfield/raw flag representation.
Search for other item decomp PRs adding HSD_JObjAddRotationY users to validate the inline helper pattern.
Search for subsequent renames of itheart functions or HeartContainerVars fields once semantics became clearer.
Check whether the BobOmbRain.x0 type correction from enum_t to HSD_GObj* was reused or refined in later item PRs.
Look for additional review discussion about C bitfields causing mismatches in item variable structs.
